query: We're looking at a diversification rule: no single crop's production output should exceed 20% of total crop tonnage. What does the optimizer return for income and the cropping mix?
ground_truth_constraint:
xprod[cotton-h]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[banana]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sugar-cane]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-arr]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-cor]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[oranges]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[manioc]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[corn]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sisal]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
optichat_constraint:
xprod[cotton-h]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[banana]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sugar-cane]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-arr]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-cor]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[oranges]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[manioc]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[corn]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sisal]  <=  0.2*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])

query: Temporary labor spikes in peak months while family labor sits idle elsewhere. Shouldn't each month's temporary labor stay under 10% of the family-plus-temporary labor for that month? What's keeping the model from making that move?
ground_truth_constraint:
tlab[jan]  <=  0.1*(tlab[jan] + flab[jan])
tlab[feb]  <=  0.1*(tlab[feb] + flab[feb])
tlab[mar]  <=  0.1*(tlab[mar] + flab[mar])
tlab[apr]  <=  0.1*(tlab[apr] + flab[apr])
tlab[may]  <=  0.1*(tlab[may] + flab[may])
tlab[jun]  <=  0.1*(tlab[jun] + flab[jun])
tlab[jul]  <=  0.1*(tlab[jul] + flab[jul])
tlab[aug]  <=  0.1*(tlab[aug] + flab[aug])
tlab[sep]  <=  0.1*(tlab[sep] + flab[sep])
tlab[oct]  <=  0.1*(tlab[oct] + flab[oct])
tlab[nov]  <=  0.1*(tlab[nov] + flab[nov])
tlab[dec]  <=  0.1*(tlab[dec] + flab[dec])

query: Total labor cost looks high relative to what we bring in. Is there a reason the plan doesn't hold total labor cost at no more than a quarter of total revenue? Wouldn't that ratio produce a healthier-looking plan?
